title: Triple-Pedestal Farmhouse Table
summary: A long farmhouse dining table on three separate arched-corbel pedestals tied by a center stretcher — three of this catalog's single-pedestal table technique in a row, on six leveling feet.
description:
This table extends the single-pedestal idea to real dining-table length by building three identical pedestals rather than trying to span the whole table on one oversized post — each pedestal gets its own decorative arched corbels, jigsawed and sanded smooth before attaching, then a base board and a top board that anchor it to the tabletop and floor. Build all three pedestal legs first, since they're identical, and keep outside edges flush and glued at every joint — a nail gun genuinely speeds this step up if you have one.

Leveling feet are a real, non-decorative feature here: six small feet under the three pedestals mean a single uncooperative foot can be shimmed on its own rather than fighting the whole table for level, which matters more on a three-pedestal table than a one-pedestal table simply because there are three separate contact points that all need to agree with the floor.

A center stretcher runs the length of the table, joining all three pedestals into one rigid understructure before the tabletop goes on — this is the step that turns three independent pedestals into a single long table rather than three side tables pushed together. Build the tabletop separately with a pocket-hole jig, add the decorative aprons with glue, then set the whole top down onto the assembled base.

materials.name: Primer and paint, or stain and poly

cutList.note: Both ends beveled at 45°, long point to long point, ends not parallel.
Cut into an arch shape with a jigsaw.
Both ends beveled at 30° off square, long point to long point, ends not parallel.

steps.title: Build the three pedestal legs
steps.body: Build three identical leg assemblies from the leg tops and supports, keeping outside edges flush and using glue. Make sure the stretcher board fits into the middle opening of each.

steps.title: Add the top and bottom feet
steps.body: Add the top and bottom feet boards to each leg assembly.

steps.title: Add the decorative corbels
steps.body: Cut the corbels with a jigsaw and sand smooth before attaching to each pedestal.

steps.title: Add the base board
steps.body: Attach the base board to the bottom of each pedestal.

steps.title: Attach the top board
steps.body: Attach the top board on top of each pedestal with screws and glue.

steps.title: Add leveling feet
steps.body: Attach leveling feet pads to the bottom of each pedestal, six total, so any uncooperative foot can be shimmed on its own.

steps.title: Add the center stretcher
steps.body: Position all three pedestals in place and attach the center stretcher, joining them into one rigid base.

steps.title: Build the tabletop
steps.body: Build the tabletop from the 1x6 boards edge to edge with a pocket-hole jig, then attach the end and side trim.

steps.title: Add the decorative aprons
steps.body: Attach the apron trim pieces to the underside of the tabletop with glue.

steps.title: Attach the base and finish
steps.body: Set the tabletop on top of the assembled pedestal base and attach with screws and glue, predrilling to avoid splitting. Sand and finish.
materials: Primer and paint, or stain and poly

unresolvedImages.alt: Long farmhouse dining table on three arched-corbel pedestals
